items.title: Myc overexpression improves recovery from myocardial infarction associated with cardiomyocyte hyperplasia in the mouse heart
items.author: Miguel Torres
items.content: Moderate overexpression of Myc promotes heart function recovery after ischemia, leading to functional and morphological improvements with a hyperplastic phenotype through diploid cardiomyocyte proliferation and generation of new binucleated cardiomyocytes.

items.title: Overlapping upstream ORFs ending at c.125 lead to reduced Endoglin, contributing to Hereditary Hemorrhagic Telangiectasia
items.author: Omar Soukarieh
items.content: In silico and functional analysis of ENG 5’UTR variants altering upstream ORFs contributes to the molecular diagnosis of Hereditary Hemorrhagic Telangiectasia and clears the way for the interpretation of this kind of variants in human diseases.

items.title: Bivalent single-domain antibodies show potent mpox virus neutralization through M1R antigen
items.author: Koichi Watashi
items.content: This study demonstrates that multimerization of single-domain antibodies (VHHs) targeting MPXV M1R significantly enhances neutralizing activity. the approach offers a modular platform for engineering next-generation antivirals against orthopoxviruses.

items.title: Assisting and accelerating NMR assignment with restrained structure prediction
items.author: Yi Qin Gao
items.content: AI-driven RASP model and FAAST pipeline utilize experimental information to aid multi-domain or few-MSA protein structure prediction and accelerate accurate NMR NOESY assignments, advancing integrative structural biology.

items.title: Long-term potentiation-induced changes in actin dynamics and spine geometry persist on the timescale of the synaptic tag
items.author: Michael Fauth
items.content: A mathematical model of dendritic spine membrane interacting with dynamic and stable actin exhibits LTP-induced alterations on the timescale of the synaptic tag due to up-regulation of stable actin which are also observed in FRAP experiments in vitro.

items.title: A murine model lacking Lyst recapitulates Chediak-Higashi syndrome with an earlier-onset neurodegenerative phenotype
items.author: May Christine V. Malicdan
items.content: A Lyst knockout mouse model of Chediak-Higashi Syndrome recapitulates human neurological disease, providing new insights into its neuropathophysiology and a robust platform for therapeutic development.

items.title: Structure and post-translational modification of the prostaglandin transporter
items.author: Benjamin J. Orlando
items.content: Cryo-EM and functional studies of the human prostaglandin transporter reveal its overall topology and show that extracellular disulfide bonds are critical for proper trafficking and prostaglandin transport activity.

items.title: Author Correction: Evolutionary roots of the risk of hip fracture in humans
items.author: Hila May
items.content: Author Correction: Evolutionary roots of the risk of hip fracture in humans
